subject: biology
title: 19M.3.SL.TZ1.14
question_html: Explain what is meant by competitive exclusion, with respect to a named example of an invasive species.
solution_html:
a. when two species with similar niches occupy the same habitat ✔

b. the competition increases
OR
reduction in resources ✔

c. the population of one species will die/be excluded ✔

d. an invasive species «often» lacks predators ✔

e. the endemic species is usually the one that cannot compete ✔

f. eg: grey squirrel/Japanese knot weed/cane toad ✔

[3 max] if the invasive species is not named.

Do not allow humans.
examiners_html: This was generally well answered. There were a wide variety of verifiable invasive species and only a few confused examples. The most common example was the cane toad introduced into Australia. Candidates understood increased competition and lack of predation.
